other_observers: none even though several of us tried to refind the bird the rest of the day and the next day
specific_location: 24247 Highway 287 5 miles south from the edge of Lamar and almost half mile to west in backyard
description: -a little larger and stockier than a Western Kingbird -striking striped head pattern (is what caught my attention) -black crown with strong white line below -strong black stripe from bill to back of head below the white stripe -fairly heavy bill -white throat -yellow belly -plain rufous back with tail and wings more reddishBehavior: -stood on the rocks by the water feature -stood in the very very shallow water where water moves over rocks between two ponds -fed on insects? from water  -have some photos of food items in mouth that will be sent to David Leatherman to determine what it might be - the entire time was spent in this locationCall: never heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp
